title: Instrumentality The Only Hope
wikitext:
[]: Instrumentality: The Only Hope?             Quote Post by Tumbling Down » Wed Jul 09, 2014 9:08 am http://wiki.evageeks.org/FGC:Episode_02_Cut_028 “Precisely. Under these desperate circumstances, that project is our one and only hope.” Wait, so Instrumentality was being done in order to save humanity? That doesn't make any sense. Aren't the plans of SEELE what endangered humanity in the first place?

[]:             Quote Post by Sorrow » Wed Jul 09, 2014 9:13 am Because someone says it's to save humanity, doesn't mean that's the case. Many people who do evil things believe it is for a noble cause. Seele's cause might actually be noble, they're after the eradication of pain and loneliness within people. The problem (read evil) is that they are speaking for the entirety of the human race. As is proof in the show: some people value individuality more than being pain free. Seele are forcing their good onto everyone, which is cruel.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Wed Jul 09, 2014 9:16 am In Seele's view humanity is doomed to either destroy itself or wither away at an 'evolutionary dead end' unless they can guide us all to apotheosis before the finite resources available to us are exhausted. From this perspective Instrumentality really is the only way out, and everything else is just the high cost to be paid to make it happen. Whether or not they're actually right about that... well, that's a whole other discussion.

[]:             Quote Post by Tumbling Down » Wed Jul 09, 2014 1:11 pm Thanks! That makes things so much clearer.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Wed Jul 09, 2014 1:20 pm Seele's religious nonsense kept humanity from a great diapsora into the stars aboard Sahaquiel-derived warships armed with Ramiel-derived laser cannons, invading other worlds weaing Zeruel-armor and armed with angelic weaponry. They had a goddamn instruction manual to, and they flipped to the page that says "don't do this" and then did that.

[]:             Quote Post by Tumbling Down » Wed Jul 09, 2014 6:37 pm Chuckman wrote: They had a goddamn instruction manual to, and they flipped to the page that says "don't do this" and then did that.

[]:             Quote Post by Firetrout » Wed Jul 09, 2014 6:39 pm Tumbling Down wrote: Wait, what?

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Wed Jul 09, 2014 10:12 pm I'm not sure any of that was really an option Chuckman (angels want to reboot the world and shit, yo), but it would make for a decent fanfic.

[]:             Quote Post by Tumbling Down » Thu Jul 10, 2014 5:50 am Firetrout wrote: The Dead Sea Scrolls.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Thu Jul 10, 2014 6:35 am NemZ wrote: I'm not sure any of that was really an option Chuckman (angels want to reboot the world and shit, yo), but it would make for a decent fanfic.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Thu Jul 10, 2014 10:23 am Serial Experiments Lain uses the same "evolutionary dead end" concept -- it is probably Lost Decade malaise at work.

[]:             Quote Post by EvangelionGodMode » Fri Jul 11, 2014 7:20 am Wait so i was wondering did seele control the angels and when and where they came from?

[]:             Quote Post by Sorrow » Fri Jul 11, 2014 8:16 am EvangelionGodMode wrote: Wait so i was wondering did seele control the angels

[]:             Quote Post by EvangelionGodMode » Sat Jul 12, 2014 12:12 am ok what about kaworu?

[]:             Quote Post by Sorrow » Sat Jul 12, 2014 1:36 am I believe he was made through human contact with Adam. It's not explained entirely, but he was born the same day that Adam was awoken and then put into an embryonic state. It seems to be that Kaworu was a result of a human contacting Adam directly, the human DNA merged with Adam's soul and Kaworu was born, presumably as a new-born? Though, I think this out-come was more or less desired SEELE, therefore Kaworu was created by SEELE. If you're wondering why Kaworu "works" for SEELE: that would be because they raised him, fed him information on their goal etcetera; exactly as Rei was. They're not slaves to the organisations however, merely mislead, uninformed or given a false sense of existence.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Sat Jul 12, 2014 2:01 am Kaworu is an angel-hybrid clone of Misato's dad. Or possibly Misato herself. Basically if he'd stopped by the apartment and said 'hey yo I'm your daddy and or you' he could have gone to the luckiest place on earth but he'd rather shower with Shinji instead.

[]:             Quote Post by Sorrow » Sat Jul 12, 2014 2:07 am It sounds like you mean "being with Misato would be extremely fortuitous" when, in what you wrote, he was aware he was Misato's dad/or Misato herself. It was the "better option than showering with Shinji". Do you think he would be so kinky? At any rate, could you point me towards the evidence for him being Misato's dad/Misato? Surely if that were the case, he wouldn't have been there to send his daughter off or she wouldn't have been there to be sent off? Or does natural contact simply take/copy DNA from the human as opposed to their whole being? Why would it take the Angel's entire soul? So many more questions..

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Sat Jul 12, 2014 2:12 am Some people, and by some people I mean me, think that Misato was in Antarctica for Second Impact to try and pilot Adam. Because the thing you do when you find a sleeping alien god from beyond the moon is shove a fourteen year old girl in its neck. I'm pretty sure most of the forum dismisses it so I guess she went to the worst Take Your Daughter to Work Day ever. Misato's mom must have been a real treat. "Hey mom can I have some money to go to the movies" "No you little slut, I'm sending you to Antarctica"

[]:             Quote Post by Sorrow » Sat Jul 12, 2014 2:18 am That's quite a lot of assumptions. I never said, I never said they were completely unfounded. It's certainly an interesting idea, but I was always of the opinion that if it's enough to remove Adam's soul in its entirety then it would surely be enough to cause the person to cease existing, thus resulting in the new hybrid as the replacement of both. I liked your colourful interpretation of Misato's life, but compare that to one of the other children we see in the series and it's just another Tuesday, really.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sat Jul 12, 2014 5:23 am Chuckman wrote: Some people, and by some people I mean me, think that Misato was in Antarctica for Second Impact to try and pilot Adam. Because the thing you do when you find a sleeping alien god from beyond the moon is shove a fourteen year old girl in its neck.
